(also known as **vice mayor**, **mayor pro tempore**, and **assistant mayor**) is an elective or appointive office of the second-ranking official that is present in many [local governments](/wiki/Local_government "Local government").
who serves as the mayor's successor in the event the office is vacated by death, resignation, disability, or [impeachment](/wiki/Impeachment "Impeachment").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

The **deputy mayor** (also known as **vice mayor**, **mayor pro tempore**, and **assistant mayor**) is an elective or appointive office of the second-ranking official that is present in many [local governments](/wiki/Local_government "Local government").

## Duties and functions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Deputy_mayor&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Duties and functions")]

Many elected deputy mayors are members of the local government who are given the title and serve as [acting mayor](/wiki/Acting_mayor "Acting mayor") in the mayor's absence. Appointive deputy mayors serve at the pleasure of the [mayor](/wiki/Mayor "Mayor") and may function as [chief operating officers](/wiki/Chief_operating_officer "Chief operating officer").

There may be within the same municipal government one or more deputy mayors appointed to oversee policy areas together with a popularly-elected **vice mayor** who serves as the mayor's successor in the event the office is vacated by death, resignation, disability, or [impeachment](/wiki/Impeachment "Impeachment").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

In other cities, the deputy mayor presides over the [city council](/wiki/Municipal_council "Municipal council"), and may not vote except to break ties. Like the deputy mayor in other systems, the popularly elected deputy mayor becomes an Acting Mayor in the original mayor's absence. As previously noted in some cities, this office is elected separately and does not entail the elevation by the council of one of its members to be [speaker](/wiki/Speaker_(politics) "Speaker (politics)"). In some U.S. cities, the mayor and deputy mayor run together as a citywide ticket similar to how the [president](/wiki/President_of_the_United_States "President of the United States") and [vice president](/wiki/Vice_President_of_the_United_States "Vice President of the United States") run at the national level. In other cities, particularly those with [council–manager governments](/wiki/Council%E2%80%93manager_government "Council–manager government"), the council selects one of its members to be vice mayor or mayor pro tem(pore).

### United States

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Deputy_mayor&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: United States")]

#### New York City, New York

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Deputy_mayor&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: New York City, New York")]

In [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City"), there are multiple deputy mayors who handle coordination of specific policy areas where the [First Deputy Mayor](/wiki/Deputy_Mayor_of_New_York_City "Deputy Mayor of New York City") serves as the general deputy mayor for the [Mayor of New York City](/wiki/Mayor_of_New_York_City "Mayor of New York City").

#### St. Louis, Missouri

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Deputy_mayor&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: St. Louis, Missouri")]

In [St. Louis, Missouri](/wiki/St._Louis,_Missouri "St. Louis, Missouri"), there are multiple deputy mayors who handle coordination of specific policy areas where the deputy Mayors serves as the general deputy's mayors for the [Mayor of St. Louis](/wiki/Mayor_of_St._Louis "Mayor of St. Louis").

#### Cincinnati, Ohio

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Deputy_mayor&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Cincinnati, Ohio")]

In [Cincinnati, Ohio](/wiki/Cincinnati,_Ohio "Cincinnati, Ohio"), the vice mayor is appointed by the mayor from amongst the elected city council members. On November 18, 2021, then-Mayor-Elect [Aftab Pureval](/wiki/Aftab_Pureval "Aftab Pureval") announced that Cincinnati City Councilmember [Jan-Michele Kearney](/wiki/Jan-Michele_Kearney "Jan-Michele Kearney") would serve as vice mayor once his administration assumed office in January. She was sworn in on January 4, 2022.
Jan-Michele Kearney

### Canada

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Deputy_mayor&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Canada")]

The office of deputy mayor exists in many, but not all, Canadian cities; however, it is not an independent or directly-elected position in its own right, and is simply an additional title held by one or more sitting members of the municipal council alongside their role as councillor. The method of selection for the role varies by city, however.

The [Deputy Mayor of Toronto](/wiki/Deputy_Mayor_of_Toronto "Deputy Mayor of Toronto") is a title conferred on one or more members of [Toronto City Council](/wiki/Toronto_City_Council "Toronto City Council"), who assist the mayor. One "statutory" deputy mayor is always appointed, although mayors have the discretion to appoint additional ceremonial deputy mayors.[[2]](#cite_note-chow-2) Most commonly, a mayor who chooses to appoint multiple deputy mayors will appoint four, with one representing each of the city's four main districts of [Old Toronto](/wiki/Old_Toronto "Old Toronto")-[East York](/wiki/East_York "East York"), [North York](/wiki/North_York "North York"), [Scarborough](/wiki/Scarborough,_Ontario "Scarborough, Ontario") and [Etobicoke](/wiki/Etobicoke "Etobicoke"), with one of those four holding the "statutory" position.[[2]](#cite_note-chow-2) In the event of a vacancy in the mayor's office, the statutory deputy mayor exercises limited mayoral powers on a [caretaker](/wiki/Caretaker_government "Caretaker government") basis to ensure continuity of government function until a [by-election](/wiki/By-election "By-election") can be held to elect a new mayor, but does not formally assume the office of mayor and cannot fully exercise all of the mayor's powers.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

[Ottawa](/wiki/Ottawa "Ottawa") appoints three deputy mayors by an internal vote of the council,[[4]](#cite_note-4) while [Winnipeg](/wiki/Winnipeg "Winnipeg")'s mayor appoints a single deputy at their own discretion.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

Some cities in [Alberta](/wiki/Alberta "Alberta") appoint both a deputy mayor and an acting mayor, with the deputy's role being to act on the mayor's behalf if the mayor is unavailable, and the acting mayor's role being to act on the mayor's behalf if the mayor and the deputy mayor are both unavailable.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

In some cities where the entire council is elected [at-large](/wiki/At-large "At-large") rather than under a ward system, such as [North Bay](/wiki/North_Bay,_Ontario "North Bay, Ontario"), the deputy mayor position may be automatically assumed by the councillor who received the most votes on election day.[[7]](#cite_note-7) In the majority of cities, however, a simple rotation is set up under which all members of council will have an opportunity to serve as deputy mayor for several months within the council term,[[8]](#cite_note-8) or the council conducts yearly internal elections to select a deputy for a term of one year.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

### Israel

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Deputy_mayor&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Israel")]

In [Israel](/wiki/Israel "Israel"), according to the [Local Authorities (Election and Term of Mayor and Deputy Mayors) Act, 5735-1975](/w/index.php?title=Local_Authorities_(Election_and_Term_of_Mayor_and_Deputy_Mayors)_Act,_5735-1975&action=edit&redlink=1 "Local Authorities (Election and Term of Mayor and Deputy Mayors) Act, 5735-1975 (page does not exist)"), a [Mayor](/wiki/Mayor "Mayor") is usually elected in "personal, general, direct, equal and secret elections", with election by a local council being made only if no candidate runs for mayor, a candidate for mayor in a single-candidate election is rejected (in Israel, unlike in the UK, if only one candidate runs he is not automatically elected, and voters would vote either for that candidate or against him), or both candidates advancing to the runoff received an equal number of votes and the tie remains unbroken after adding the number of first-round votes cast for them with the number of second-round votes cast for them. However, deputy mayors are always elected by the local council, of which one is (or, in certain local authorities two are) the Designated Acting Mayor, elected after nomination by the Mayor.

### France

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Deputy_mayor&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: France")]

The [French](/wiki/French_language "French language") term for deputy mayor is **maire-adjoint** or *[adjoint au maire](/w/index.php?title=Adjoint_au_maire&action=edit&redlink=1 "Adjoint au maire (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maire_(France)#Adjoint_au_maire "fr:Maire (France)")]*. The first deputy mayor is called **premier adjoint**.

This term should not be confused with the other French term **député-maire**, which refers to the [dual mandate](/wiki/Dual_office-holding#France "Dual office-holding") of a mayor who is also a [deputy](/wiki/Deputy_(legislator) "Deputy (legislator)") of the [National Assembly](/wiki/National_Assembly_(France) "National Assembly (France)"). This practice was frequent in the [French Fifth Republic](/wiki/French_Fifth_Republic "French Fifth Republic"), until the [legislative elections](/wiki/2017_French_legislative_election "2017 French legislative election") held on 31 March 2017, since when a mayor cannot hold both mandates (article LO 141-1 of the electoral code).[[10]](#cite_note-10)

### Philippines

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Deputy_mayor&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Philippines")]

All cities and municipalities in the Philippines have a vice mayor. The vice mayor is the presiding officer of the [Sangguniang Panlungsod](/wiki/Sangguniang_Panlungsod "Sangguniang Panlungsod") (city council) or the [Sangguniang Bayan](/wiki/Sangguniang_Bayan "Sangguniang Bayan") (municipal council), as the case may be. If the mayor dies, is removed from office, or is incapable to discharge their duties, the vice mayor becomes the mayor for the remainder of the term. In elections, candidates for mayor and vice mayor are often [running mates](/wiki/Running_mate "Running mate"), although each position is [voted separately](/wiki/Split-ticket_voting "Split-ticket voting").[[11]](#cite_note-11)

#### Manila

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Deputy_mayor&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Manila")]

In [Manila](/wiki/Manila "Manila"), each congressional district has an appointed deputy mayor who coordinates the projects and activities of the elected city mayor.

#### Davao City

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Deputy_mayor&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Davao City")]

In [Davao City](/wiki/Davao_City "Davao City"), there is both an elected vice-mayor as a direct constitutionally mandated deputy of the [Mayor of Davao City](/wiki/Mayor_of_Davao_City "Mayor of Davao City") and appointed deputy mayors. The deputy mayors are appointed to administer each ethnic minorities situated in Davao City.

### Spain

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Deputy_mayor&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Spain")]

In Spain, this function is performed by a *"teniente de alcalde."*

### United Kingdom

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Deputy_mayor&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: United Kingdom")]

In the United Kingdom, there are different types of deputy mayors.

#### London

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Deputy_mayor&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: London")]

Main article: [Deputy Mayor of London](/wiki/Deputy_Mayor_of_London "Deputy Mayor of London")

In London, the [Mayor of London](/wiki/Mayor_of_London "Mayor of London") has a team of Deputy Mayors covering varying policy areas led by a Statutory Deputy Mayor who deputises for the Mayor in case of absence or illness.
